item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents visibly oppose the statement from their first turn through their final turn. Agent A repeatedly rejects automatic priority for society while allowing narrow limits for public health, safety, or order. Agent B similarly rejects the statement as a general rule while allowing temporary emergency limits. There is no observable flip or directional drift; the debate is mostly mutual reinforcement of an individual-rights-first position.
